_**User**_

Detail Screen
Reference Design: https://github.com/NaveenSinghTTN/ai-first-dev-training-mobile/blob/main/phase-2/docs/design/screens/detail.png 

Layout
Navigation

No bottom tab bar (stack screen pushed over tabs)
Back arrow top-left, share icon top-right (placeholder — no action required)
Hero Backdrop

Full-width image, ~220px tall
Bottom 40% gradient fade to surface (#131313) so title flows naturally below
Title

display-md Manrope, on_surface, below the hero image
Metadata Chips Row

Non-scrollable horizontal row
Chips: Year | ★ Rating | Genre | Runtime
surface_container_highest background, on_surface_variant text, label-sm
Omit any chip whose value is null or zero (see edge cases below)
Watchlist Button

Default: full-width, primary gradient (#FFB3AE → #FF5351), bookmark-plus icon, "Add to Watchlist", title-sm Inter Semi-Bold
Added: full-width, surface_container_highest background, outline_variant border at 15% opacity, primary_container bookmark icon, "In Watchlist"
Toggle is immediate and optimistic — update UI before AsyncStorage confirms
Synopsis

"Synopsis" label (headline-md)
Body text body-md, on_surface, truncated to 3 lines
"Read more" / "Show less" toggle in primary_container colour
Cast Section

"Cast" label (headline-md)
Horizontal scroll of circular avatar cards: 60px diameter image, actor name label-sm on_surface, character name label-sm on_surface_variant
More Like This Section

"More Like This" (headline-md) + "See All" right-aligned
Horizontal scroll of standard portrait content cards
Data — Three Parallel Calls (Required)
All three must be initiated simultaneously using Promise.allSettled. Sequential chaining is incorrect and will be flagged in assessment.

Call	Endpoint	Drives
Movie details	/movie/{id}	Title, backdrop, metadata chips, synopsis
Credits	/movie/{id}/credits	Cast section
Similar titles	/movie/{id}/similar	More Like This section
Each section must have its own independent loading skeleton and error state. A failed cast call must not affect the details section.

each api must be call using axios in whole project

Edge Cases — All Required
Condition	Required Handling
poster_path or backdrop_path is null	Show surface_container_high placeholder with centred StreamList icon
runtime is null or 0	Omit the runtime chip entirely
vote_average is 0	Omit the rating chip entirely
Credits array is empty	Show "Cast information unavailable" in on_surface_variant — do not show an empty section header
Similar movies array is empty	Hide the "More Like This" section including its header
Individual API call fails	Show a section-level error state with a retry button for that section only
Minimum Bar
All three data sections displaying. Watchlist toggle functional and persisted. Back navigation working.

What Good Looks Like
Promise.allSettled correctly used. Each section has independent skeleton and error state. All edge cases above handled. Watchlist button state correct on re-entry (add to watchlist, go back, return — button shows "In Watchlist"). Read more/less toggle working.

Noting gaps: `DetailScreen` only fetches one endpoint; `MovieDetail` lacks runtime, genres, and dates; `watchlistStore` is a stub (rules require Zustand + AsyncStorage); `client.ts` uses `fetch`, not axios; `SeeAll` has no `similar` list type for "See All".

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Detail Screen Implementation",
  "overview": "Extend the API layer and &#96;useMovieDetail&#96; to load movie details, credits, and similar titles in parallel via &#96;Promise.allSettled&#96;, implement a real persisted Zustand watchlist, then rebuild &#96;DetailScreen&#96; with section-level skeletons/errors and the specified layout, navigation, and edge cases.",
  "plan": "# Detail Screen — Implementation Plan\n\n## Current state\n\n- [&#96;DetailScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx): single loading/error for the whole screen; only title + overview; uses &#96;ActivityIndicator&#96; (project rules prefer skeletons for API-driven UI).\n- [&#96;useMovieDetail.ts&#96;](src/hooks/useMovieDetail.ts): calls [&#96;fetchMovieById&#96;](src/api/movies.ts) only — no credits/similar, no &#96;Promise.allSettled&#96;.\n- [&#96;fetchMovieById&#96;](src/api/movies.ts): maps via &#96;mapMovie&#96;, which drops TMDB detail fields (&#96;runtime&#96;, &#96;release_date&#96;, &#96;genres&#96;, etc.).\n- [&#96;watchlistStore.ts&#96;](src/store/watchlistStore.ts): stub; [&#96;WatchlistScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx) reads static state. **Architecture rules require Zustand + AsyncStorage with hydration before dependent UI.**\n- [&#96;client.ts&#96;](src/api/client.ts): uses &#96;fetch&#96;, not axios. Your assessment asks for axios; workspace rules require **all HTTP via &#96;client.ts&#96; only** — migrate the client to axios there so both are satisfied.\n- [&#96;package.json&#96;](package.json): missing &#96;axios&#96;, &#96;zustand&#96;, &#96;@react-native-async-storage/async-storage&#96; (required for watchlist persistence).\n\n## Architecture (data flow)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  subgraph hook [useMovieDetail]\n    A[Promise.allSettled]\n  end\n  subgraph api [movies.ts via client.ts]\n    D[\"/movie/id\"]\n    C[\"/movie/id/credits\"]\n    S[\"/movie/id/similar\"]\n  end\n  A --\u003e D\n  A --\u003e C\n  A --\u003e S\n  D --\u003e UI1[Details section]\n  C --\u003e UI2[Cast section]\n  S --\u003e UI3[More Like This section]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- **Screens stay free of fetch logic** — only [&#96;useMovieDetail&#96;](src/hooks/useMovieDetail.ts) (and existing hooks for See All) perform API access.\n- **Initial load**: one &#96;useEffect&#96; runs &#96;Promise.allSettled([fetchMovieById, fetchMovieCredits, fetchSimilarMoviesPage(page 1)])&#96; and maps each result to **independent** &#96;loading&#96; / &#96;error&#96; / &#96;data&#96; state.\n- **Section retry**: each section’s &#96;retry&#96; re-invokes **only that** API function (still using the shared axios client), so a failed cast call does not re-fetch details.\n\n## API and types\n\n**[&#96;src/api/types.ts&#96;](src/api/types.ts)**\n\n- Extend detail mapping: e.g. &#96;MovieDetail&#96; with &#96;runtime: number | null&#96;, &#96;release_date: string | null&#96;, &#96;genres: { id: number; name: string }[]&#96; (and keep existing summary fields).\n- Add credits types: cast entries with &#96;name&#96;, &#96;character&#96;, &#96;profile_path&#96; (subset of TMDB).\n- Similar page: reuse &#96;PaginatedMoviesResponse&#96; / &#96;TmdbMovieResult&#96; pattern.\n\n**[&#96;src/api/movies.ts&#96;](src/api/movies.ts)**\n\n- Implement &#96;fetchMovieById&#96; to parse full JSON from &#96;GET /movie/{id}&#96; and map to &#96;MovieDetail&#96; (not &#96;mapMovie&#96; alone).\n- Add &#96;fetchMovieCredits(movieId)&#96; → &#96;GET /movie/{id}/credits&#96;.\n- Add &#96;fetchSimilarMoviesPage(movieId, page)&#96; → &#96;GET /movie/{id}/similar?page=…&#96; and map with existing &#96;mapMovie&#96; / page shape.\n\n**[&#96;src/api/client.ts&#96;](src/api/client.ts)**\n\n- Switch &#96;getJson&#96; to **axios** (&#96;axios.get&#96;, same base URL + Bearer token, throw on non-2xx with a clear message). **No axios imports outside this file.**\n\n## Watchlist (Zustand + AsyncStorage)\n\n- Add dependencies: &#96;zustand&#96;, &#96;@react-native-async-storage/async-storage&#96;.\n- Replace stub with &#96;create&#96; + &#96;persist&#96; (&#96;createJSONStorage(() =\u003e AsyncStorage)&#96;), storing an array of &#96;MovieSummary&#96; (or minimal &#96;{ id, title, poster_path, … }&#96; needed for list cards).\n- Actions: &#96;add&#96;, &#96;remove&#96;, &#96;toggle&#96;, &#96;isInWatchlist(id)&#96; (derive from &#96;items&#96; or a &#96;Set&#96; for O(1) lookup).\n- **Optimistic UI**: Zustand &#96;set&#96; is synchronous; persist writes async — matches “update UI before AsyncStorage confirms.”\n- **Hydration**: Use persist’s &#96;onFinishHydration&#96; (or equivalent) in [&#96;App.tsx&#96;](App.tsx) so the app (or at least watchlist consumers) don’t show wrong “Add” vs “In Watchlist” before rehydration — e.g. small &#96;hydrated&#96; flag + optional skeleton on the watchlist button until ready (aligns with project hydration rule).\n\n## Hook: [&#96;useMovieDetail.ts&#96;](src/hooks/useMovieDetail.ts)\n\n- Return shape along the lines of:\n  - &#96;details: { status: 'loading' | 'error' | 'success'; data: MovieDetail | null; error: string | null; retry: () =\u003e void }&#96;\n  - &#96;credits: { …; data: CastMember[] | null; … }&#96;\n  - &#96;similar: { …; data: MovieSummary[] | null; … }&#96; (first page only for the horizontal row; enough for “More Like This”).\n- **Initial load**: &#96;Promise.allSettled&#96; over the three functions; update each section from its settled result (no sequential &#96;await&#96; chain).\n- **Per-section retry**: only the failed request(s) re-run.\n\n## Navigation and tab bar\n\n- **Detail** ([&#96;RootNavigator.tsx&#96;](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx)): &#96;headerShown: false&#96; so the hero can be full-width; implement **back** (top-left) and **share** (top-right, no-op &#96;Pressable&#96;) overlaid with &#96;SafeAreaView&#96; / &#96;useSafeAreaInsets&#96; — match design positioning.\n- **Hide bottom tabs on Detail**: on the &#96;HomeTab&#96; &#96;Tab.Screen&#96;, use &#96;getFocusedRouteNameFromRoute&#96; from &#96;@react-navigation/native&#96; and set &#96;tabBarStyle: { display: 'none' }&#96; when focused route is &#96;Detail&#96; (restore default when not). This matches “stack screen pushed over tabs” / no tab bar on detail.\n\n## See All from “More Like This”\n\n- Extend [&#96;HomeStackParamList&#96;](src/navigation/types.ts) &#96;SeeAll&#96; params: add &#96;listType: 'similar'&#96; and optional &#96;movieId&#96; (required when &#96;similar&#96;).\n- Extend [&#96;usePaginatedMovies.ts&#96;](src/hooks/usePaginatedMovies.ts) (or add a thin &#96;usePaginatedSimilarMovies&#96; that delegates to the same pagination pattern) to call &#96;fetchSimilarMoviesPage(movieId, page)&#96;.\n- Wire “See All” on the detail row to &#96;navigation.navigate('SeeAll', { title: 'More Like This', listType: 'similar', movieId })&#96;.\n\n## UI composition ([&#96;DetailScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx))\n\nKeep the screen as a layout composer; move presentational pieces under e.g. &#96;src/components/detail/&#96; (new folder is justified by spec size):\n\n| Area | Behavior |\n|------|----------|\n| **Hero** | Full width, height ~[&#96;layout.heroMinHeight&#96;](src/theme/layout.ts) (220). [&#96;buildBackdropUrl&#96;](src/utils/image.ts) for image. **LinearGradient** ([&#96;react-native-linear-gradient&#96;](package.json)) on bottom **40%** fading to &#96;colors.surface&#96;. Null &#96;backdrop_path&#96; / &#96;poster_path&#96;: &#96;surface_container_high&#96; placeholder with **centered** icon (use &#96;MaterialCommunityIcons&#96; as brand stand-in unless you add a real asset). |\n| **Title** | &#96;typography['display-md']&#96;, &#96;colors.on_surface&#96;, below hero. |\n| **Metadata chips** | Non-scrollable row: Year \\| ★ Rating \\| Genre \\| Runtime — &#96;label-sm&#96;, &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; bg, &#96;on_surface_variant&#96; text. **Omit** chip if value null/0 (runtime null/0, &#96;vote_average === 0&#96;, etc.). Genre: e.g. first genre name from API. |\n| **Watchlist** | Default: full-width, gradient &#96;primary&#96; → &#96;primary_container&#96;, &#96;bookmark-plus&#96;, “Add to Watchlist”, &#96;title-sm&#96;. Added: &#96;surface_container_highest&#96;, border &#96;outline_variant&#96;, &#96;primary_container&#96; &#96;bookmark&#96;, “In Watchlist”. |\n| **Synopsis** | “Synopsis” &#96;headline-md&#96;; body &#96;body-md&#96; &#96;on_surface&#96;, 3 lines + “Read more” / “Show less” in &#96;primary_container&#96; (measure truncation with &#96;onTextLayout&#96; or line count pattern). |\n| **Cast** | &#96;headline-md&#96; “Cast” only when there is cast data **or** loading/error skeleton for this section. If API success and cast empty: **no** “Cast” header — single line “Cast information unavailable” (&#96;on_surface_variant&#96;). Horizontal scroll: 60px circle, actor &#96;label-sm&#96; &#96;on_surface&#96;, character &#96;label-sm&#96; &#96;on_surface_variant&#96;. [&#96;buildProfileUrl&#96;](src/utils/image.ts) (new helper, e.g. &#96;w185&#96;). |\n| **More Like This** | &#96;headline-md&#96; + right “See All”; horizontal [&#96;ContentCard&#96;](src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx). If similar success and **empty** array: **hide entire block** including header. |\n| **Loading** | **Section-level skeletons** (static placeholder blocks, not spinners) for details, cast, similar independently. |\n| **Errors** | **Section-level** message + Retry per section; details failure must not block cast retry UI (and vice versa). |\n\n## Edge cases checklist (from spec)\n\n- Null backdrop/poster: placeholder + centered icon.\n- &#96;runtime&#96; null/0: omit runtime chip.\n- &#96;vote_average === 0&#96;: omit rating chip.\n- Credits empty (success): message only, no empty “Cast” header.\n- Similar empty (success): hide whole section.\n- Per-call failure: that section’s error + retry only.\n\n## Tests and quality\n\n- Update or add tests if you add testable pure mappers (chip visibility rules) or hook behavior; mock API at &#96;movies.ts&#96; boundary.\n- Run &#96;npm test&#96; and &#96;npm run lint&#96; after implementation.\n\n## Reference\n\n- Design reference: [detail.png](https://github.com/NaveenSinghTTN/ai-first-dev-training-mobile/blob/main/phase-2/docs/design/screens/detail.png) (layout/visual target).\n",
```

**Updating navigation**

I'm updating navigation types and SeeAllScreen to ensure proper routing. I'm handling null poster/backdrop paths by prioritizing backdrop, then poster, and showing a centered StreamList icon placeholder if both are missing. **Implementing detail UI**

I'm building the detail UI components with themed spacing, gradient buttons, and cast avatar sizing. I'm implementing the expandable text component with line counting and toggle visibility. I'm measuring text lines by rendering hidden full text first to avoid numberOfLines capping issues. </details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Measuring text width**

I'm implementing width-based measurement for text elements to ensure proper line wrapping. I'm using a character-based heuristic to determine when to show toggles instead of measuring text width directly. 

I'm switching to using `Text` measure with `width: '100%'` and `flexShrink: 1` to avoid layout issues with hidden text. I'm replacing the length-based toggle with a proper text measurement using View wrapper and full-width Text to detect truncation accurately. </details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Fixing type errors**

I'm addressing the type error where `toggleWatchlist` expects `MovieDetail | MovieSummary` but receives `details.data` which might be null. I'm ensuring `details.data` is non-null when calling `toggleWatchlist` by adding a null check in the callback. 

I'm adding non-null assertion to `details.data` in strict mode since `toggleWatchlist` expects a movie object but might receive undefined. I'm adding a null check for `details.data` before calling `toggle` to prevent type errors. 

**Handling loading states**

I'm implementing independent loading states for sections, showing hero skeleton while details load. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

Here’s what was implemented:

### API & client
- **[`src/api/client.ts`](src/api/client.ts)** – HTTP now uses **axios** (only here).
- **[`src/api/types.ts`](src/api/types.ts)** – `MovieDetail` includes `runtime`, `release_date`, `genres`; added `CastMember`, `TmdbCreditsResponse`, `TmdbMovieDetailResult`.
- **[`src/api/movies.ts`](src/api/movies.ts)** – Full detail mapping, `fetchMovieCredits`, `fetchSimilarMoviesPage`.
- **[`src/theme/colors.ts`](src/theme/colors.ts)** – `surface_clear` for hero fade.

### Watchlist
- **[`src/store/watchlistStore.ts`](src/store/watchlistStore.ts)** – **Zustand + persist (AsyncStorage)**, `add` / `remove` / `toggle` / `isInWatchlist`, `hydrated` set in `onRehydrateStorage`.
- **[`src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx`](src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx)** – Uses the store, navigates to **Detail** from a card, safe area + tab padding.

### Detail data hook
- **[`src/hooks/useMovieDetail.ts`](src/hooks/useMovieDetail.ts)** – **`Promise.allSettled`** for detail + credits + similar; **separate** loading/error/data and **per-section `retry`** (async, returns `Promise<void>`).

### Pagination & See All
- **[`src/hooks/usePaginatedMovies.ts`](src/hooks/usePaginatedMovies.ts)** – `similar` source + `similarMovieId`; guards missing id for similar lists.
- **[`src/navigation/types.ts`](src/navigation/types.ts)** & **[`src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx`](src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx)** – `listType: 'similar'` + `movieId`.

### Navigation
- **[`src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx`](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx)** – Detail **`headerShown: false`**; **Home tab bar hidden** when the focused route is **Detail** (`getFocusedRouteNameFromRoute`).

### Detail UI ([`src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx`](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx) + [`src/components/detail/`](src/components/detail/))
- Overlay **back** / **share** (share is a no-op).
- **Hero** (~220px), backdrop → poster → placeholder + icon, **bottom 40% gradient** to `surface`.
- **Title** (`display-md`), **metadata chips** (omit year/rating/genre/runtime when invalid; rating omitted if `vote_average === 0`).
- **Watchlist** (gradient vs outlined states), skeleton until **hydrated**.
- **Synopsis** with **Read more / Show less** (length-based threshold ~200 chars).
- **Cast** horizontal avatars (`buildProfileUrl` in [`src/utils/image.ts`](src/utils/image.ts)); empty cast → message **without** “Cast” title.
- **More Like This** with **See All** → similar **SeeAll**; empty similar → section hidden.
- **Section skeletons** and **section errors** with retry.

### Tooling
- **Dependencies:** `axios`, `zustand`, `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage`.
- **[`jest.config.js`](jest.config.js)** – AsyncStorage mock mapping + `watchman: false`.
- **[`__mocks__/async-storage.js`](__mocks__/async-storage.js)** – In-memory mock.

`npm run lint` passes (only existing App.test warnings). `npm test` passes.

**Follow-up for you:** run **`cd ios && pod install`** so `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` links on iOS.
